detail: idle ambient tilt ported in CardView.ts (worktree `balatro-idle-tilt`) — virtual cursor orbits each card center (radius 0.5*ambient_tilt of card dims, per-card freq 1.56+(ID/1.14212)%1 rad/s, phase ID/1.35122) feeding the existing warp shader path; ticker runs always so stopHover blends from real cursor into orbit instead of snapping flat. typecheck + lint clean. dev server already running on user's side — visual verify on hot reload.
text:
Got source. Idle = virtual cursor orbits card center at radius `0.5*ambient_tilt*dim` (~10% of card), freq ~1.5-2.5 rad/s per card ID. Same warp shader, just mouse_pos drifts. Implementing now.

Worktree caught up. Now implement idle ambient tilt in CardView.

Now apply edits. Add idle constants, per-card ID, idle ticker logic that runs continuously.

Already read it. Continuing.

Edit went through. Continuing with handleHoverTick to drive idle and prevent stopping.

Now drop the unused `isHoverAtRest` method to avoid TS unused warning.

Also drop unused `BALATRO_HOVER_AT_REST_EPSILON`.

Typecheck + lint clean. Read final state to verify.

Dev port already in use (user's session). Typecheck + lint pass. Commit.

result: idle ambient tilt ported in CardView.ts (worktree `balatro-idle-tilt`) — virtual cursor orbits each card center (radius 0.5*ambient_tilt of card dims, per-card freq 1.56+(ID/1.14212)%1 rad/s, phase ID/1.35122) feeding the existing warp shader path; ticker runs always so stopHover blends from real cursor into orbit instead of snapping flat. typecheck + lint clean. dev server already running on user's side — visual verify on hot reload.

detail: i dont see any idle animation on the card, be aware that id should happen on or without hover, did you check balatro's source code?

cherry-picked idle ambient tilt (`83b8adc` on `new`) into the live dev checkout — HMR should now reload the card view with the orbiting virtual cursor (radius ~10% of card dim, per-card freq/phase from balatro's ID formula). Effect is subtle (~1-2% corner warp, matching balatro's own idle), visible as gentle breathing on every card with no hover required.
text:
Likely dev server runs in main checkout — my worktree edits aren't visible there. Let me verify.

Dev runs in main checkout, not worktree. Apply commit there.

Cherry-picked to main checkout's `new` branch — dev's HMR should pick it up now. The dev server runs in `/home/jofre/projects/luminous_ui`, not the worktree, so my initial worktree commit was invisible.

result: cherry-picked idle ambient tilt (`83b8adc` on `new`) into the live dev checkout — HMR should now reload the card view with the orbiting virtual cursor (radius ~10% of card dim, per-card freq/phase from balatro's ID formula). Effect is subtle (~1-2% corner warp, matching balatro's own idle), visible as gentle breathing on every card with no hover required.

detail: the idle animation plays now, but why are they zoomed in?

lowered idle warp to 0.25 (from 0.8) — keeps the orbital wobble visible while removing balatro's baseline corner bulge that read as zoom against our previously-flat resting state. Applied to dev's `new` branch (commit `e3030c4`) for HMR.
text:
Let me re-check balatro's mouse_damping which scales the warp.

The "zoom" is balatro's baseline warp — its shader always runs at hovering=1, so even idle cards bulge ~1-2% from corner expansion. Our app's prior baseline was flat (no warp), so the change feels like cards grew. Lower idle amount.

Edit applied. Commit + sync to main checkout.

result: lowered idle warp to 0.25 (from 0.8) — keeps the orbital wobble visible while removing balatro's baseline corner bulge that read as zoom against our previously-flat resting state. Applied to dev's `new` branch (commit `e3030c4`) for HMR.

detail: no, the problem is that the card is displaced on idle, this sould only happen when hovering the card
idle warp set to 0 (commit `86b6844` on `new` for HMR) — cards now rest rigid at idle, only warping/displacing on hover.
text:
Got it. The idle warp distorts the card silhouette (perceived as displacement) because corners get pushed asymmetrically. Drop idle warp to 0 — orbit math keeps running for smooth hover-out blend, but mesh stays flat at idle.

result: idle warp set to 0 (commit `86b6844` on `new` for HMR) — cards now rest rigid at idle, only warping/displacing on hover.

Worth flagging: balatro's idle *is* the orbiting warp (Card:draw runs the shader at hovering=1 always, mouse position orbits center). What you saw as "displaced" matches what balatro itself does — but on our flat baseline it reads as wrong. With idle warp disabled there's effectively no idle animation now. If you want some other idle motion (e.g. a slow scale breathe, subtle bob/float), let me know which direction.

detail: `new` already contains the three equivalent commits via cherry-pick (`83b8adc`, `e3030c4`, `86b6844`); worktree removed since its commits were SHA-duplicates of those.
text:
Main checkout's `new` already has all three commits via cherry-pick (`83b8adc`, `e3030c4`, `86b6844`). Worktree commits are equivalent content with different SHAs — nothing to merge. Exit worktree.

result: `new` already contains the three equivalent commits via cherry-pick (`83b8adc`, `e3030c4`, `86b6844`); worktree removed since its commits were SHA-duplicates of those.

detail: i dont see the idle animation

idle warp re-enabled with orbital differential — at idle, the warp from the orbiting cursor has the mouse-at-card-center baseline subtracted and both axes recentered, so the static zoom/translation cancel out and only the corner shape morph renders. Commit `51529c2` on `new` (HMR). Cards should now visibly wobble in shape at rest without translating or growing.
text:
Re-enable idle warp but force recenter when not hovering — centroid stays pinned at home so silhouette doesn't translate, but corners can still wobble asymmetrically as the virtual cursor orbits. Visible without "displacement".

Now find updatePerspective and force recenter when not hovering.

Recenter alone won't fix the baseline zoom (symmetric corner expansion). Need to also subtract the static baseline warp so only the orbital differential renders. Let me add that.

Implement: at idle, subtract a mouse-at-card-center baseline warp from the orbital warp, so only the differential shape morph renders. No zoom, no translation, but the wobble is visible. Bump warp amount higher since we're filtering the baseline.

Now update updatePerspective to subtract baseline at idle.

result: idle warp re-enabled with orbital differential — at idle, the warp from the orbiting cursor has the mouse-at-card-center baseline subtracted and both axes recentered, so the static zoom/translation cancel out and only the corner shape morph renders. Commit `51529c2` on `new` (HMR). Cards should now visibly wobble in shape at rest without translating or growing.
